summary: Inventory the 7 pages not covered in the first sweep of thefairytails.co.uk

pages.title: Intensive Dog and Puppy Training (meta title) / H1: "Intensive dog training with dog board and train"

sections.heading: Intensive Training (hero carousel)
sections.summary: Intro hero with image carousel/slider featuring training imagery. Slides include 'For adult dogs and puppies' (promotes puppy and adult dog programs) and 'TAR Training (Train – Adjust – Repeat)' introducing the methodology.

sections.heading: Our Intensive Training plans use the TAR training method
sections.summary: Overview explaining TAR is repetition-based training performed at a suitable distance/duration with controlled distractions.

sections.heading: TAR training (Train – Adjust – Repeat)
sections.summary: Detailed explanation of the TAR method: repeat-treat-adjust cycle, linked to conditioned emotional response (with in-page anchor link to the CER section). Notes the approach is suitable for a maximum of 2 behavioural issues.

sections.heading: How our Intensive Training plans work
sections.summary: Four-step process: 1) Contact, 2) Approve estimate, 3) Training begins, 4) Training ends.

sections.heading: How does it help?
sections.summary: Five subsections covering: session length, consistency, environmental exposure, gradual increase of distractions, and reward consistency.

sections.heading: Pricing
sections.summary: Two pricing tiers — Intensive Puppy Training (£1200/1 month) and Intensive Dog Training (£1800/2 months, +£300 large dogs) — each with a Contact us button.

sections.heading: Some of our doggy clients in training
sections.summary: Gallery carousel of client dogs with short testimonial-style captions: New experiences, Learning development, Developing skill set, Keeping focused. Has a 'View more' carousel control (no URL).

sections.heading: Conditioned Emotional Response (CER)
sections.summary: Educational/science section (anchor id ConditionedEmotionalResponse) explaining CER: classical conditioning, brain mechanisms (neurons, amygdala), and counterconditioning.

pages.title: The Fairy Tails Dog Day School (page covers Puppy Training Classes)

sections.heading: Small group evening classes for you and your puppy
sections.summary: Intro section: group puppy training classes held at Concordia Hall, St. Leonards on Thursday evenings at 6pm, covering basic commands, socialisation, and owner-puppy bonding. Contains the (stale) line: "For 2024, classes will be held at Concordia Hall, St. Leonards on Thursday evenings at 6pm."

sections.heading: Why choose group puppy training classes?
sections.summary: Five subsections: 'Start training early' (recommends starting before 6 months old); 'Two dog trainers' (at least two trainers, positive reinforcement); 'What you will learn' (sit, stay, come, nutrition, advanced commands, long lead training, day school sessions, hand signals, dog law, body language); 'What's included' (states "four sessions spread over one month plus two intro sessions to The Fairy Tails dog day school"); 'Socialisation' (exposure to environments, people, other dogs).

sections.heading: How will your pup benefit?
sections.summary: Benefit subsections with icons: 'Enjoyable classes' (short puppy attention spans need positive experiences); 'Building confidence' (variety of stimuli); 'Professional trainers' (structured guided training); 'A strong foundation' (early obedience); plus paragraphs on 'Building a bond', 'Understanding your pup', and 'A positive experience' about positive reinforcement and mutual understanding.

sections.heading: How to book your place
sections.summary: Pricing and booking section. Offers online booking via Acuity or contacting the business; also cross-sells the DIY puppy course.

pages.title: Puppy Course - DIY (H1 hero: "Puppy Course DIY")

sections.heading: Puppy Course DIY (hero/H1)
sections.summary: Page intro for a free do-it-yourself puppy training course resource.

sections.heading: Aims
sections.summary: Lists the course goals: socialisation exercises, toilet training, separation (anxiety) training, sleep training, enrichment, and obedience skills including walks and socialisation outings.

sections.heading: Methods
sections.summary: Explains the positive-reinforcement training philosophy as scientifically effective, using treats, toys, and praise to encourage desired behaviours while building puppy confidence.

sections.heading: Management Methods in Training?
sections.summary: Covers environmental controls (crates, gates, leashes) to set puppies up for success and prevent unwanted behaviours without punishment.

sections.heading: Use of Punishment
sections.summary: Warns punishment may give quick results but risks creating anxiety, fears, phobias, and lack of confidence, potentially producing reactive dogs instead of confident learners.

sections.heading: Training Schedule Suggestion
sections.summary: Recommends six daily training touchpoints integrated with feeding and toilet breaks, plus a detailed hour-by-hour daily schedule running roughly 5-7am ('Wake up and Toilet Time'), 7am breakfast, 7.15 toilet break, through to 8pm 'Relaxation and wind down'.

sections.heading: Toilet Training
sections.summary: Recommends toilet breaks every 15 minutes initially, with frequency decreasing as the puppy matures and becomes more reliable. Links to a separate Puppy Toilet Schedule page.

sections.heading: Week One - Week Four modules (H3s)
sections.summary: A progressive 4-week curriculum with one linked sub-page per week: Week 1 'Skills & Exposure', Week 2 'Build Confidence', Week 3 'Socialisation', Week 4 'Fine Tuning'.

pages.title: Dog Training Resources (free training-resources/education page for The Fairy Tails K9 Centre)

sections.heading: Hero carousel (3 cards): 'Free Training Resources' / 'For adult dogs and puppies' / 'Dog Training & Puppy Training Resources'
sections.summary: Three rotating hero cards introducing the free training resources offering; third card has a 'Start Today' label. All carousel buttons are non-functional placeholders with no href — caption text is also placeholder in places.

sections.heading: Empowering Dog Owners Through Education and Data
sections.summary: Intro copy: successful dog ownership requires knowing how to train effectively and communicate with your dog, with the whole family involved in consistent training.

sections.heading: Training the Whole Family
sections.summary: Dog parents must educate the entire household on setting rules and encouraging positive behaviour, avoiding teasing or inconsistent approaches.

sections.heading: Using Data to Train Effectively
sections.summary: Research-backed reinforcement methods and correct reward timing build trust between owner and dog.

sections.heading: Creating Positive Change Through Knowledge
sections.summary: Educating yourself about dog training improves the relationship and helps navigate common ownership challenges.

sections.heading: The Importance of Puppy Classes
sections.summary: Puppy classes provide 'fun, off-leash experiences' for socialisation across different environments and situations.

sections.heading: Why Dog Training Is Different Today
sections.summary: Contrasts modern, flexible pet-dog training with traditional working-dog methods; focus on real-life behavioural situations rather than rigid commands.

sections.heading: Building a Strong Relationship with Your Dog
sections.summary: Frames training as relationship-building centred on 'communication, trust, and making sure your dog is a well-adjusted, happy part of your family.'

sections.heading: Lets Get a Head Start (Puppy Training Resources — 4-step process)
sections.summary: Step process: 1) Before Getting Puppy — contact via phone/email/contact form; 2) On Puppy Arrival — plan preparation and approval; 3) Training DIY Home Course — implementation; 4) Training - Maintain it — handoff once satisfied.

sections.heading: Dog Training Resources (4-step process)
sections.summary: An identical 4-step process block mirroring the puppy version with the same steps — duplicate content a rebuild could consolidate.

sections.heading: Some of our doggy clients in training (gallery)
sections.summary: Four gallery items: New experiences, Learning development, Developing skill set, Keeping focused. Includes a 'View more' button with no href (placeholder/JS-only).

sections.heading: Conditioned Emotional Response (CER)
sections.summary: Long-form educational deep dive on CER: dogs forming emotional associations with stimuli, Pavlovian conditioning, neurological detail (amygdala), and counterconditioning that 'changes past connections between neurons.'

pages.title: The Fairy Tails — Staged Dog Training (training stages page)

sections.heading: Staged Dog Training / Our Dog Training Course: A Three-Stage Approach
sections.summary: Intro framing the training programme as a three-stage approach: integration, basic training in a quiet setting, then real-world environment exposure.

sections.heading: Stage 1
sections.summary: Integration phase, roughly one week long, focused on gently introducing the dog to the daycare environment and acclimating it to the facility.

sections.heading: Stage 2
sections.summary: About a week of basic training in a quiet, distraction-free setting around the barn, giving the dog space and tranquillity to master essential commands and behaviours.

sections.heading: Stage 3
sections.summary: Final stage trains in busier real-world environments: on-lead at local dog parks and off-lead areas (dog stays on-lead for training), the seafront promenade, and bustling high streets.

sections.heading: Parent Training
sections.summary: From the fourth week onwards, parent (owner) training sessions are included as part of the third phase, typically on Fridays for boarding and intensive-training dogs, with pickup between 11 AM and 2 PM; mid-course and end-of-course sessions are recommended.

pages.title: The Fairy Tails — Drop-Off and Pick-Up Information for Dog Boarding

sections.heading: Drop-Off and Pick-Up Information for Dog Boarding
sections.summary: Sole body section (H1 + image). Copy: 'We're excited to welcome your dog to stay with us!' — drop-off/pick-up service is available on weekdays; on weekends owners must bring and collect dogs themselves. Boarding drop-off/pick-up windows are 8:00am-11:00am weekdays and 8:30am-11:00am weekends; later pick-ups must be arranged in advance or an additional day of boarding charges applies; bookings must be paid at time of reservation. Closes with 'We look forward to making your dog's stay as comfortable and enjoyable as possible!'
